contract.name: v2/disaster/def_code/count.md
contract.description: Returns the count of Disaster Emergency Fund (DEFC) codes from the request that currently have disaster/emergency funding.

filter.description: Container for search criteria. Omitting it or providing null yields a 422 validation error. The backend only evaluates the def_codes array; other keys are accepted but ignored.

filter.def_codes.description: Array of uppercase DEFC strings to evaluate. Items must be one of ['1','2','3','4','5','6','7','8','9','A','AAA','AAB','AAC','AAD','AAE','AAF','AAG','AAH','AAI','AAJ','AAK','AAL','B','C','D','E','F','G','H','I','J','K','L','M','N','O','P','Q','QQQ','R','S','T','U','V','W','X','Y','Z']. Duplicates are allowed but deduplicated.
filter.def_codes.constraints: required; minItems=1; items must belong to the observed enum

count.description: Integer count of distinct requested DEFC that currently have disaster/emergency funding. Returns 0 when none qualify; duplicates in the request do not increase the value.

quirks: Request payload must be sent as JSON with Content-Type application/json; otherwise the server reports the required field as missing.
Duplicate DEFC entries in def_codes do not increase the returned count because the endpoint deduplicates before tallying.
Filter keys other than def_codes (for example, time_period or agencies) are accepted but ignored, even for future-only ranges.
Invalid DEFC values trigger a 400 response that echoes the set of allowed codes.
risks: Clients must send Content-Type application/json; otherwise the body is ignored and the request fails validation.
Invalid DEFC values trigger 400 responses, so integrations should validate codes before calling.
Returned counts depend on live disaster funding data and can change between calls with identical inputs.
Additional filters such as time_period are silently ignored, which can mislead callers into assuming results are constrained.
gaps: The authoritative DEFC enumeration may change over time; only the set currently returned in 400 responses has been verified.

probes.notes: Same payload without Content-Type header is ignored and fails validation.
Filter object without def_codes fails validation.
Empty def_codes array rejected for being below minimum size.
Invalid DEFC is rejected with a 400 and the allowable enum.
Duplicate codes return a count of 1, demonstrating deduplication.
Future-only time_period filter is accepted but does not change the count.
Valid DEFC that currently lacks funding returns 0.
Mixing valid and invalid DEFC yields a 400; the valid code is not processed.

gaps: The authoritative DEFC enumeration may change over time; only the set currently returned in 400 responses has been verified.
risks: Clients must send Content-Type application/json; otherwise the body is ignored and the request fails validation.
Invalid DEFC values trigger 400 responses, so integrations should validate codes before calling.
Returned counts depend on live disaster funding data and can change between calls with identical inputs.
Additional filters such as time_period are silently ignored, which can mislead callers into assuming results are constrained.
